provenanceRelations.description: Knowledge statement cites a source section.
Knowledge is formally defined in a source section.
Knowledge is supported by source evidence.

commonRelations.description: A is used to analyze B.
A and B are conceptually equivalent.
A provides a model basis for B.
A is required before learning or applying B.
A and B are semantically related.

mathematics.description: A can be transformed into B under valid rules.

physics.description: A is valid in regime/domain B.
A is experimentally verified by B.
